message:
fix(service): deduplicate alternating wakers in Buffer, gate CxProvider behind tower feature

Buffer service waker deduplication:
- Extract push_waker_if_new() helper that checks all registered wakers
  via will_wake() before appending. The previous approach only compared
  against the last waker, which meant two alternating callers (A, B, A,
  B) would accumulate unbounded duplicate entries—one pair per poll
  cycle—eventually degrading wake performance on high-contention paths.
- Add two regression tests (poll_ready_deduplicates_alternating_waiters
  and waiting_for_ready_deduplicates_alternating_inner_waiters) that
  exercise the exact alternating-waiter pattern.

CxProvider tower feature gating:
- Replace #[allow(dead_code)] annotations on CxProvider, ThreadLocalCxProvider,
  and FixedCxProvider with proper #[cfg(feature = "tower")] gates. These
  types are part of the Tower service adapter API surface and should only
  compile when the feature is active—dead_code allows were masking the
  fact that non-tower builds were paying for unused code.
- Move the CxProvider test block into a cfg(feature = "tower") submodule
  so tests remain in sync with the gating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
